taskTitle: Fix structural in settings-ui: If settings-ui components directly mutate project-root-models types rather than  (+4 more)

input.prompt:
Explore the gotobed Swift project structure. I need to understand:
1. The full directory layout under Sources/
2. The contents of these files (read them fully):
   - Any MenuContent.swift
   - Any Palette.swift
   - Any SettingsWindowController.swift
   - Any AppEnvironment.swift
   - Package.swift
3. What zone structure exists (Scheduler, Overlay, UI/Settings zones)
4. How AppEnvironment wires things together

Search breadth: very thorough. Read all relevant Swift files in full.

input.description: Run make validate to confirm clean starting state
Remove old SettingsWindowController.swift from UI/ (moved to composition-root level)

input.old_string:
# Zone map (directory → role):
#
#   Sources/GoToBed/Scheduler/    service layer   — pure scheduling logic, no UI
#   Sources/GoToBed/Overlay/      overlay-ui      — presentation only; no settings or scheduler
#   Sources/GoToBed/UI/           settings-ui     — settings views; no overlay or scheduler
#   Sources/GoToBed/AppEnvironment.swift
#                                 app-lifecycle   — composition root; only file allowed
#                                                   to wire all zones together
input.new_string:
# Zone map (directory → role):
#
#   Sources/GoToBed/Scheduler/           service layer   — pure scheduling logic, no UI
#   Sources/GoToBed/Overlay/             overlay-ui      — presentation only; no settings or scheduler
#   Sources/GoToBed/UI/                  settings-ui     — settings views; no overlay or scheduler
#   Sources/GoToBed/AppEnvironment.swift
#   Sources/GoToBed/SettingsWindowController.swift
#                                        app-lifecycle / composition root — the only files
#                                        allowed to wire all zones together.
#                                        SettingsWindowController lives here (not in UI/)
#                                        because window lifecycle is a composition-root concern.

input.old_string:
# Types whose definition lives in Sources/GoToBed/UI/
SETTINGS_UI_TYPES=(
    AppearanceEditor
    ScheduleEditorView
    SettingsView
    SettingsWindowController
    WeekdayPicker
)
input.new_string:
# Types whose definition lives in Sources/GoToBed/UI/
SETTINGS_UI_TYPES=(
    AppearanceEditor
    ScheduleEditorView
    SettingsView
    WeekdayPicker
)

# Types whose definition lives at the composition-root level alongside
# AppEnvironment.swift (Sources/GoToBed/*.swift, not in any subdirectory zone).
# Scheduler and Overlay zones must not reach into the composition root.
COMPOSITION_ROOT_TYPES=(
    SettingsWindowController
)

input.old_string:
# Rule 1: Scheduler zone must not reference overlay or settings-ui types.
#         The scheduler is a pure service; UI concerns must never leak in.
check_excludes "Sources/GoToBed/Scheduler" "scheduler-zone" \
    "${OVERLAY_TYPES[@]}" "${SETTINGS_UI_TYPES[@]}"

# Rule 2: Overlay zone must not reference settings-ui types or the scheduler.
#         The overlay is a presentation component wired by the composition root;
#         it must remain independent of settings views and scheduling logic.
check_excludes "Sources/GoToBed/Overlay" "overlay-zone" \
    "${SETTINGS_UI_TYPES[@]}" "${SCHEDULER_TYPES[@]}"
input.new_string:
# Rule 1: Scheduler zone must not reference overlay, settings-ui, or
#         composition-root types. The scheduler is a pure service; UI and
#         windowing concerns must never leak in.
check_excludes "Sources/GoToBed/Scheduler" "scheduler-zone" \
    "${OVERLAY_TYPES[@]}" "${SETTINGS_UI_TYPES[@]}" "${COMPOSITION_ROOT_TYPES[@]}"

# Rule 2: Overlay zone must not reference settings-ui, composition-root types,
#         or the scheduler. The overlay is a presentation component wired by the
#         composition root; it must remain independent of settings and scheduling.
check_excludes "Sources/GoToBed/Overlay" "overlay-zone" \
    "${SETTINGS_UI_TYPES[@]}" "${COMPOSITION_ROOT_TYPES[@]}" "${SCHEDULER_TYPES[@]}"

input.description: Build the project to validate all changes compile correctly
Run full validation: build, tests, scripts
Stage all changes and show status

input.content:
Fix structural issues in settings-ui zone (5 findings)

Five SourceVision findings addressed:

1. **Leaky store writes** — All direct `Store` mutation calls in settings-ui
   views (ScheduleEditorView, SchedulesPane, DefaultAppearancePane) are replaced
   by coordinator methods on `AppEnvironment` (`updateSchedule`, `addSchedule`,
   `deleteSchedule`, `makeSchedule`, `updateDefaultAppearance`). Views now hold
   read-only references to Store for SwiftUI reactivity; all writes flow through
   the composition root.

2. **MenuContent co-location / domain coupling** — `MenuContent` and its inner
   `ScheduleRow` no longer hold `@EnvironmentObject var store: Store`.
   `AppEnvironment` now relays `store.objectWillChange` to its own publisher
   (via Combine sink) so menu views stay reactive. A new `ScheduleDisplayItem`
   value type (`MenuViewModel.swift`) carries pre-computed display strings into
   the menu; `ScheduleRow` receives it as a plain value plus an `onToggle`
   callback, removing every direct domain dependency from the menu view layer.

3. **Palette visual tokens** — `BrandColor`, the `ColorComponents(hex:)` /
   `matchesRGB` extensions, and `AppearancePreset` are moved to
   `GoToBedCore/Models/Palette.swift` (public, no UI imports). They are now
   accessible to any current or future UI zone without a cross-zone import.
   `GoToBed/UI/Palette.swift` retains only the SwiftUI views `PresetRow` and
   `SwatchPicker`.

4. **SettingsWindowController location** — `SettingsWindowController.swift` is
   moved from `Sources/GoToBed/UI/` to `Sources/GoToBed/` (alongside
   `AppEnvironment.swift`), signalling that window lifecycle is a
   composition-root concern, not a settings-ui view concern.

5. **Zone layering script** — `check-zone-layering.sh` updated: `SettingsWindowController`
   removed from `SETTINGS_UI_TYPES` and added to a new `COMPOSITION_ROOT_TYPES`
   list; Rules 1 & 2 now also exclude composition-root types from
   scheduler/overlay zones.

All 59 tests pass. `make validate` green.

input.resolutionDetail: Moved Palette tokens to GoToBedCore, moved SettingsWindowController to composition root, introduced MenuViewModel/ScheduleDisplayItem, added AppEnvironment coordinator methods for all store writes, removed direct Store write dependencies from all settings-ui views and MenuContent.

input.detail:
All 5 settings-ui structural findings addressed:

(1) Leaky store writes: Added coordinator methods to AppEnvironment (updateSchedule, addSchedule, deleteSchedule, makeSchedule, updateDefaultAppearance, setScheduleEnabled). ScheduleEditorView, SchedulesPane, and DefaultAppearancePane now route all writes through env; store is still injected for reactive reads only.

(2) MenuContent domain coupling: Removed @EnvironmentObject var store: Store from MenuContent and ScheduleRow entirely. AppEnvironment now relays store.objectWillChange via Combine sink so env-only observers stay reactive. New ScheduleDisplayItem value type in MenuViewModel.swift carries pre-computed display strings; ScheduleRow takes it as a plain value + onToggle callback.

(3) Palette visual tokens: Moved BrandColor, ColorComponents(hex:)/matchesRGB extensions, and AppearancePreset to GoToBedCore/Models/Palette.swift (public, no UI imports). GoToBed/UI/Palette.swift now contains only PresetRow and SwatchPicker SwiftUI views.

(4) SettingsWindowController location: Moved from Sources/GoToBed/UI/ to Sources/GoToBed/ alongside AppEnvironment.swift, marking it as a composition-root concern.

(5) Zone layering enforcement: Updated check-zone-layering.sh — SettingsWindowController removed from SETTINGS_UI_TYPES, added to new COMPOSITION_ROOT_TYPES; Rules 1 & 2 now exclude composition-root types from scheduler/overlay zones.

All 59 tests pass. make validate green.

summary:

### 1. Leaky store writes → AppEnvironment coordinator
